purpose: Mitigation playbook. For each risk pattern, what mitigations actually work, what mitigations look reassuring but don't, ranked by effectiveness and feasibility. Use in DD §11.3 Renegotiation Levers and as input to retainer scope conversations.
principle: A risk register without mitigation analysis is incomplete. Junior DD lists risks. Principal DD ranks mitigations.

structure.what_works: Mitigations Anton has seen execute and deliver outcome on real assets.
structure.looks_reassuring_but_doesnt_work: Mitigations that appear in management presentations but fail in execution. These are the trap.
structure.deal_structure_levers: Buyer-side mechanisms that price or contain risk without operational fix.

power_contract_risk_aluminium.risk: PPA expiry within DD horizon, merchant exposure, tariff escalation

what_works.mitigation: Renegotiate PPA pre-close with extended term and capped escalation
what_works.feasibility_for_buyer: medium — requires power counterparty cooperation, often regulatory approval
what_works.evidence_anton_seen: RUSAL Siberian asset renegotiation 2016-2018, multi-year process but achievable

what_works.mitigation: Captive power development (greenfield or acquisition)
what_works.use_case: Only when asset acquisition price reflects power vulnerability and buyer has capital depth

what_works.mitigation: Power hedge via long-dated forward contracts
what_works.effectiveness: medium — caps downside but not upside, basis risk to local tariff
what_works.feasibility: medium — depends on liquidity of regional power market

looks_reassuring_but_doesnt_work.mitigation: Verbal assurances from regulator or power provider
looks_reassuring_but_doesnt_work.why_it_fails: Regulators change. Power provider counterparties get nationalised, privatised, restructured. Verbal commitments don't survive personnel changes.
looks_reassuring_but_doesnt_work.frequency_seen: very high — every Russian regional asset DD has this

looks_reassuring_but_doesnt_work.mitigation: Historical stability used as forward-looking comfort
looks_reassuring_but_doesnt_work.why_it_fails: Tariff structures are revised on political cycle, not on commercial cycle. Past 5-year stability does not predict next 5 years.

deal_structure_levers: Purchase price reduction reflecting NPV of tariff stress case
Earn-out tied to actual tariff outcomes over 3y
Retention escrow for first PPA renewal event
Seller representation and warranty on PPA terms with material adverse change indemnity

potline_relining_backlog.risk: Pot age concentration creating CapEx wave inadequately provisioned

what_works.mitigation: Accelerated relining schedule with in-house cathode plant capacity expansion
what_works.feasibility: medium — requires capital and disciplined potline rotation

what_works.mitigation: Cell technology retrofit during relining (current density increase, busbar compensation)
what_works.effectiveness: high if scope right — captures CapEx for upgrade rather than just maintenance
what_works.feasibility: medium — requires engineering and trial period

looks_reassuring_but_doesnt_work.mitigation: Extended pot life claims based on individual outlier pots
looks_reassuring_but_doesnt_work.why_it_fails: Average pot life is set by failure distribution, not by best-performing pots. Extending pot life beyond design through 'good operations' usually masks deferred relining decision.

deal_structure_levers: Working capital adjustment at close to reflect imminent CapEx need
Deferred consideration mechanism tied to actual pot age at hand-over verified by independent technical review

spent_potlining_legacy_liability.risk: SPL stockpile without treatment route, tightening regulation

what_works.mitigation: Dedicated treatment facility construction with multi-asset off-take
what_works.feasibility: medium — requires capital and inter-company cooperation
what_works.evidence: Several Russian and Brazilian groups built consortium SPL plants

what_works.mitigation: Cement co-processing contracts where jurisdiction permits
what_works.effectiveness: medium — cost-effective but regulator may close loophole
what_works.feasibility: high in current regulatory regime; high tail risk on regulatory change

looks_reassuring_but_doesnt_work.why_it_fails: Storage is not treatment. Regulator can require treatment at any future point. Tail liability remains on the books.
looks_reassuring_but_doesnt_work.frequency: high — most asset SPL stockpiles fall in this category

deal_structure_levers: Specific indemnity from seller for SPL legacy liability with quantum cap
Seller responsibility for treatment route definition pre-close

carbon_intensity_cbam_exposure.risk: EU customer exposure, CBAM phase-in, customer carbon audit pressure

what_works.mitigation: Power purchase from low-carbon source (PPA shift, captive renewables)
what_works.feasibility: high in jurisdictions with PPA market; medium where regulated tariff dominant

what_works.mitigation: Customer book shift to non-EU markets
what_works.effectiveness: medium — preserves margin but loses premium VAP demand from EU automotive

what_works.mitigation: ASI (Aluminium Stewardship Initiative) certification process
what_works.effectiveness: high reputational, medium economic — opens premium customer pool willing to pay carbon premium
what_works.feasibility: medium — 18-36 month process with significant disclosure

looks_reassuring_but_doesnt_work.why_it_fails: Offsets do not count under CBAM methodology. Customer carbon disclosure requires direct intensity, not net intensity.

looks_reassuring_but_doesnt_work.mitigation: Reclassifying scope boundaries to exclude high-carbon power
looks_reassuring_but_doesnt_work.why_it_fails: CBAM methodology and ASI methodology will catch reclassification. Customers conducting audits will catch it. Short-term win, long-term credibility damage.

deal_structure_levers: Purchase price adjustment for carbon-intensity-driven cost-curve repositioning
Carbon-CapEx earmark in CapEx plan with milestones

anode_supply_quality_risk.risk: Petroleum coke quality drift driving current efficiency loss

what_works.mitigation: Diversified coke supply contracts with quality specifications and rejection rights
Anode quality testing infrastructure (rapid feedback to procurement)
what_works.feasibility: high — modest CapEx for laboratory upgrade

what_works.mitigation: Captive or anchor-supplier relationship with coke producer
what_works.effectiveness: high for stability; medium for cost optimisation

looks_reassuring_but_doesnt_work.mitigation: Long-term supply contracts without quality specifications
looks_reassuring_but_doesnt_work.why_it_fails: Contract gives volume security, not quality. Coke quality is set by upstream refinery economics that supplier controls.

tailings_storage_facility_risk.risk: Stewardship gap, upstream construction, post-Brumadinho institutional bar

what_works.mitigation: Conversion to dry stacking or paste tailings
what_works.effectiveness: very high — eliminates wet tailings risk
what_works.feasibility: low for retrofit on operating mine; medium for greenfield

what_works.mitigation: TSF closure and new downstream-construction facility
GISTM compliance program with independent governance review

looks_reassuring_but_doesnt_work.why_it_fails: Monitoring tells you something is happening; it doesn't stop it. Upstream TSFs in seismic zones cannot be made safe through monitoring alone.

deal_structure_levers: Independent technical review trigger in deal documents
Required CapEx for tailings transition as condition precedent to close

sanctions_ownership_cascade.risk: UBO chain sanctions exposure, secondary sanctions for non-Russian buyer

what_works.mitigation: UBO restructuring pre-close (asset spin-out, ownership reorganisation)
what_works.feasibility: medium-low — requires multi-party agreement and regulator approval
what_works.warning: Restructuring done to avoid sanctions can itself trigger evasion designation. Must be done with both Russian and target-jurisdiction sanctions counsel.

what_works.mitigation: Direct asset acquisition without inheriting corporate structure
what_works.feasibility: medium — requires asset transfer documentation and approvals

looks_reassuring_but_doesnt_work.mitigation: Trust or nominee structures masking UBO
looks_reassuring_but_doesnt_work.why_it_fails: Trusts and nominees can be designated themselves. Sophisticated regulators see through these structures.

looks_reassuring_but_doesnt_work.mitigation: Geographic re-routing of legal headquarters without UBO change
looks_reassuring_but_doesnt_work.why_it_fails: Sanctions follow the natural person, not the company seat.

deal_structure_levers: Walk-away right if any UBO chain entity is designated post-signing pre-close
Compliance condition precedent to all major milestones

deferred_maintenance_pattern.risk: Maintenance spend below norm, equipment age increasing

what_works.mitigation: Catch-up CapEx plan with 5-year horizon, prioritised by criticality
Maintenance-as-a-service contracts with OEMs for critical equipment
what_works.feasibility: medium — depends on OEM presence in jurisdiction

looks_reassuring_but_doesnt_work.mitigation: Maintenance budget increase without root-cause analysis
looks_reassuring_but_doesnt_work.why_it_fails: Money without prioritisation goes to the loudest equipment, not the most critical. Need engineering analysis first, then budget.

general_mitigation_principles.philosophy: Mitigation discipline is the test of management quality. Bidder DD discovers risks; counter-bidder DD demands credible mitigation. Asset values rise where management has named risks and shown plan; values fall where management has obscured.
questions_to_ask_management_about_mitiga: Who owns this mitigation, and what is their accountability mechanism?
What is the budget and timeline?
What are the dependencies (regulatory, supplier, capital)?
What is the leading indicator that mitigation is working, and what is the trigger for abandonment?
Has this approach been tried at this asset or peer asset, and what was the outcome?
